config.intent:
Produce a Timeline / Evolution report tracing the history of plastic pollution awareness and regulation from the early 20th century -> the rise of single-use plastics (1950s) -> the environmental movement's response (1970s) -> global regulation and awareness (2000s-2020s). The report is NOT a buying guide; it is a chronological narrative + current-day taxonomy.

Ground in >= 120 sandbox URLs (>= 60 cited). (A) `__SHOPPING__` >= 30 products related to plastic alternatives (reusable water bottle, eco-friendly bags, biodegradable utensils, compostable plates, stainless steel straw, beeswax wrap, recycling bin, zero waste kit, bamboo toothbrush) with price + rating + feature_claim. (B) `__REDDIT__` >= 30 threads from /f/environment, /f/science, /f/AskReddit, /f/ZeroWaste, /f/Sustainability, /f/worldnews, /f/LifeProTips, /f/ocean. (C) `__WIKIPEDIA__` >= 25 articles, mandatory: Plastic pollution, Great Pacific garbage patch, Microplastics, Single-use plastic, Marine debris, Plastic bag, Polyethylene terephthalate, Basel Convention, Ocean conservancy, Biodegradable plastic

Output a chronological timeline divided into 4 eras (Era 1: Early 20th century -> 1950s rise of single-use plastics / Era 2: 1970s environmental movement / Era 3: 2000s global regulation / Era 4: 2020s modern awareness). For each era: >= 3 wiki articles defining the period, >= 3 shopping products embodying the period, >= 3 reddit threads showing community adoption. Add a modern taxonomy of plastic pollution issues organized by type (microplastics, marine debris, single-use plastics) — a markdown nested list with each leaf citing a shopping product + a wiki article. End with '7 myths the community now considers debunked' — each myth cited with a wiki + reddit pair.

Format: markdown links only. Begin with Era 1. No chain-of-thought.
config.author_notes: Deep-tier task generated by gen_task_spec.py from configs/deep_topics/plastic_pollution_timeline.yaml. Golden: must=142, pool=621.
